# Daily Report — 2026-05-16

## Task Completed
Added `reopen_incident` tool to `incident_tools.py`. The tool accepts an incident number or sys_id and PATCHes the state back to New (state=1, default) or In Progress (state=2). An optional `work_notes` field lets the caller document why the incident is being reopened. Registered in the `service_desk` and `full` tool packages.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1079
- Coverage: 91%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing vulnerabilities in environment packages (pyjwt, setuptools, urllib3, wheel) — none introduced by today's change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/incident_tools.py` — added `ReopenIncidentParams` model and `reopen_incident` function
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — exported `reopen_incident`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported and registered `reopen_incident` + `ReopenIncidentParams`
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added `reopen_incident` to `service_desk` and `full` packages
- `tests/test_reopen_incident.py` — 11 new tests (sys_id path, number-lookup path, not-found, lookup error, PATCH error, work_notes, param defaults)
- `.github/DAILY_BACKLOG.md` — moved item 1 to Completed
